raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased a phone with my credit card ( Citibank ). The first phone turned out to be blocked, so the seller sent me another phone and promised that if that was ended up being blocked theyd send me another phone. After checking the new phone, it was also blocked ( that means the XXXX is reported lost or stolen ). Additionally it was repaired with non-genuine parts so I cant get insurance on it, cant get a warranty claim, and also can not trade it in. 

After sending the credit card company MANY MANY documents by physical mail ( as they requested ) and then via email, and after many phone calls where the representative promised to get me taken care of, I kept getting letter in the mail saying dispute too late or something similar. No one on the phone could tell me a ) is there a time limit? B ) how to get a refund. No one. They just said theyd take care of it and to send them the information. 

I sent warranty info from the website ( XXXX ) which doesnt really matter because XXXX refuses to provide any recourse. 

I sent proof from XXXX that the phone has non-genuine repairs. I sent proof that the XXXX is listed blocked ( lost or stolen ) by XXXX XXXX XXXX and proof that trade in value is XXXX. 

So this is clearly fraud but Citi bank wont / cant/ refuses to provide any resolution. I offered to send the phone back but they wont tell me if they would provide a refund or not. I suggested that they provide me a refund and then I would provide proof that I returned the phone. Nothing helps. And Ive already spent SO MUCH time on the phone with these guys providing documents etc Still no resolution. 

by the way a year ago I had over {$8000.00} in fraudulent charges and it took XXXX or so calls to get them all cleared up, they missed XXXX charge I had to prove to them that they missed it, they had to file an escalation ticket and it took weeks of me following up to get it resolved. 

I would not trust this company at all and as soon as XXXX done with this issue Im going to cancel my card. I spend over {$100000.00} a year with Citi cant wait to be done!
